title: Sith Meditation Sphere

The '''Sith Meditation Sphere''' was an ancient warship used by the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] for [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]. At least [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|one meditation sphere]] survived the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] and was used by both [[Lumiya's Sith]] and the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I teach apprentices to fight.|[[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|Ship]]|<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>}}
[[File:SithMeditation_Sphere.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Naga Sadow's Sith Meditation Sphere.]]

Meditation spheres were spherical attack craft ten meters in diameter. They resembled giant, floating eyeballs with batlike wings.

At the heart of the meditation sphere used by [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] was the Sith Lord's meditation chamber. The chamber had jagged walls that sloped inwards and at the center the Sith Lord would crouch and focus his dark side powers that allowed him to amplify his intentions and send them out over great distances to control his armies and ensure victory for his forces. Thanks to the Sphere, the Dark Lord could create Illusions and make them real enough to cause damage, giving him an edge in battle.

In his chamber he was able to see the whole galaxy laid out and monitor the progress of his minions. A giant [[Viewscreen/Legends|holoscreen]] on the wall displayed more detailed tactical information.

==History==
{{Quote|Once I fought. Now I educate and protect apprentices.|Ship to Ben Skywalker|<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice" />}}
Naga Sadow constructed his sphere on the secret world of [[Khar Shian]]. The vessel was destroyed in the [[Battle at Primus Goluud]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].

[[File:NagaSadowInMothership-TFotSE4.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Naga Sadow in his Meditation Sphere]]
Over time, the spheres seemed to have been replaced in use by [[Meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chambers]], some retaining the spherical shape, aboard larger Sith vessels such as [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan's]] [[Nightfall (Sith Destroyer)|''Nightfall'']].

[[Ben Skywalker]] activated and piloted [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|another Sith Meditation Sphere]] to escape [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. The ship was self-aware and guided Ben with voices over the course of many days. The ship had formerly been piloted by a female of the original [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The ship was armed with a ventral [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] and a dorsal [[magnetic accelerator]]. Ben also stated that the Meditation Sphere seems to have whatever weapons you wanted.

Once Ben had returned to the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] with the Meditation Sphere, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] gave the ship to [[Lumiya]]. Ben once again spoke with the ship, which was intensely curious as to where all the Sith had gone. After Lumiya's [[Duel on Terephon|death]] at the hands of a grief-stricken [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] the ship was taken by [[Alema Rar]].

Alema Rar used the sphere to take her to Lumiya's [[The Home|habitat]], where she found a chip with the coordinates of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. She used the data on the chip to track down the [[One Sith|Sith order]] hiding on Korriban. She then traveled to Kashyyyk and helped the ''Anakin Solo'' escape following their [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|attack]] on that world.

When [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Zekk]], and [[Jagged Fel]] tracked Alema Rar to Lumiya's former hideout, Zekk was able to call on the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to convince the sphere to leave Alema's service. The sphere left to find a truly worthy master. Soon after, it was sighted near Ziost by the crew of the [[Poison Moon (starship)|''Poison Moon'']], a One Sith [[Interceptor-class frigate/Legends|frigate]] under the command of [[Dician]].

Dician spoke with the sphere and ordered it to come with her to Korriban and serve the One Sith. The ship rejected the philosophy of the new Sith order and departed for [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]] to make contact with the [[Lost Tribe of Sith|Sith]] there and help build a [[Sith armada (Lost Tribe of the Sith)|force]] to invade the galaxy and strike at the [[New Jedi Order]]. Upon the emergence of Abeloth, Ship was forced to do her will, and was hunted by the Jedi in hopes of finding and defeating Abeloth once and for all.
